supervisor: Rob Miller, Antonis Bikakis and Luke Dickens
description: My PhD thesis explored how to model uncertainty (Probability Theory, in particular) and knowledge-producing actions in a popular narrative-based framework for Reasoning About Actions called the Event Calculus. Excerpts of my PhD thesis were published in top Logic and AI journals and conferences, including Artificial Intelligence (AIJ) and the International Conference on Logic Programming and Nonmonotonic Reasoning (LPNMR).

degree: MSc in Pure Mathematics and Mathematical Logic
thesis: Analogical Reasoning in Unary Inductive Logic
description: Final grade: Distinction (equivalent to 110/110 cum laude in the Italian grading system).

thesis: La Tesi di Church-Turing e il suo rapporto con alcuni nuovi modelli di calcolo
description: Dissertation: "La Tesi di Church-Turing e il suo rapporto con alcuni nuovi modelli di calcolo" (en: "The Church-Turing Thesis and its relation with some new computation models")
